text.title: Quality Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the quality-management methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Quality standards MUST be numeric + testable (e.g., 'p95 < 300ms') — never adjective-only ('fast', 'reliable').
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Definition of Done MUST be a binary checklist; 'mostly done' is not done.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Quality gate output MUST be deterministic (same input → same pass/fail) — judgement gates are not gates.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Defect severity (impact on system) MUST be distinguished from priority (fix order); never collapsed.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Cost of Conformance vs Non-Conformance MUST be tracked per release for retrospective.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip for pure research or throwaway prototypes.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
